name: af Another Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesk combining Swiss and Scandinavian heritage, with neutral, clean forms and prominent ink traps characteristic of contemporary neo-grotesque design.
summary: A neo-grotesque with Scandinavian inflection, pairing Swiss neutrality with prominent ink traps across nine weights and matching italics.
description: Released in 2024 by a.Foundry, it draws on both Swiss and Scandinavian type traditions to produce a clean, legible face suited to screen and print alike. Nine weights from Thin to Black each carry a matching italic, totalling 18 static styles plus a variable font. OpenType features include inferiors, superiors, ligatures, tabular figures and a stylistic set offering a more classical character set.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Another Sans as a neo-grotesk combining Swiss and Scandinavian type design heritage, offering clarity and neutrality alongside Scandinavian characteristics. Prominent ink traps are cited as a modern addition that improves legibility and gives the face a contemporary edge. The family ships with multiple OpenType features, including a stylistic set for a more classic appearance, and covers 215 languages.

name: af Accu Serif
classification_notes: A contemporary serif with a tall x-height and glyph rhythm drawn from sans-serif aesthetics, sitting between traditional serif heritage and modern digital-first design sensibilities.
summary: A contemporary serif with nine weights, matching italics and a variable font, designed with a tall x-height and rhythm borrowed from sans-serif construction.
description: Released in 2024 by a.Foundry, af Accu Serif spans 18 styles across nine weights from Thin to Black, each with an italic, plus a variable font. The tall x-height and sans-influenced glyph rhythm make it practical for screen use, while the serifs keep it anchored in traditional typographic convention. A condensed subfamily extends its range, and OpenType features include ligatures, fractions, superiors, inferiors and stylistic sets.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Accu Serif as a contemporary serif family that incorporates elements from modern sans-serif typeface design, noting its tall x-height as well-suited for digital design and its glyph rhythm as drawn from sans-serif aesthetics, while still retaining the heritage of serif type design.

name: Sted Sans Standard
classification_notes: Drawn from Danish road signage traditions of the 20th century, the letterforms reflect the humanist conventions of Nordic wayfinding typography rather than a strict geometric or grotesque model.
summary: A contemporary sans drawn from the tradition of 20th-century Danish road signage, available in nine weights with matching italics and a variable font.
description: Rooted in the typographic heritage of Danish wayfinding, the family takes its name from the Danish word 'stedsans', meaning an intuitive sense of one's surroundings. Nine weights run from Thin to Black with matching italics, totalling 18 styles, and a variable font is included. The family sits within a wider system that includes Sted Sans Narrow and Sted Sans Expanded, with coverage across 216 languages.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe Sted Sans as a contemporary synthesis of Denmark's road signage styles, drawing on the work of influential 20th-century Danish architects and graphic designers who created wayfinding typography across the country. The name references 'stedsans', a Danish concept for the intuitive understanding of one's environment, and the typeface is framed as an expression of Danish design culture and its typographic heritage.

name: af Accu Serif Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed serif with a tall x-height and rhythm drawn from sans-serif aesthetics, placing it in a contemporary category that blends traditional serif heritage with modern proportions.
summary: A condensed serif with a tall x-height and sans-serif-influenced rhythm, released in 2024 as a subfamily of the broader af Accu Serif family.
description: Spanning nine weights from Thin to Black with matching italics and a variable font option, the family is built for digital contexts where tight measures are needed without sacrificing legibility. Its tall x-height and glyph rhythm draw openly from sans-serif conventions while retaining traditional serif detailing. Broad language coverage across 215 supported Latin-based languages makes it a practical choice for multilingual publishing.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Accu Serif Condensed as a subfamily of af Accu Serif, a contemporary serif family that incorporates elements from modern sans-serif typeface design. Its tall x-height is said to suit digital design, while the glyph rhythm takes inspiration from sans-serif aesthetics, though the design still references the heritage of serif type.

name: af Sted Sans Expanded
classification_notes: A clean, expanded-width sans-serif drawing on Danish road signage traditions of the 20th century, with geometric proportions and a contemporary finish consistent with Nordic graphic design conventions.
summary: An expanded-width sans-serif in nine weights with italics, rooted in the Danish road signage typography of the 20th century.
description: Drawing on a lineage of Danish architects and graphic designers who shaped road signage across the country, this family blends those regional signage styles into a single contemporary typeface. Nine weights from Thin to Black each carry matching italics, giving 18 static styles alongside a variable font. It sits within the wider Sted Sans collection alongside Standard and Narrow cuts, covering 216 languages across Latin Extended.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe 'Stedsans' as a Danish term for the intuitive perception of one's surroundings. They note that 20th-century Danish architects and graphic designers drew on one another to create road signage typography across the country, establishing a shared typographic heritage. af Sted Sans Expanded is presented as a contemporary synthesis of those signage styles and locales, reflecting Danish design culture.

name: af Sted Sans Narrow
classification_notes: A narrow grotesque sans-serif drawing on the tradition of Danish road signage typography from the 20th century, with clean, neutral letterforms typical of Northern European sign and wayfinding typefaces.
summary: A narrow grotesque inspired by 20th-century Danish road signage traditions, offered in nine weights with matching italics and a variable font.
description: Part of a.Foundry's Sted Sans trilogy alongside Standard and Expanded widths, this narrow cut draws on the signage typography produced by Danish architects and graphic designers throughout the 20th century. Nine weights run from Thin to Black, each with a corresponding italic, covering 216 languages across an extensive Latin Extended character set. Available as a variable font alongside static OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Sted Sans Narrow as a contemporary amalgamation of Danish road signage styles, taking its name from the Danish word 'stedsans' — the intuitive sense of one's surroundings and attentiveness to place. They draw a line from influential 20th-century Danish architects and graphic designers who created signage typography that now forms a visible typographic heritage across Denmark.

name: af Accu Serif Narrow
classification_notes: A contemporary serif with a tall x-height and rhythm drawn from sans-serif aesthetics, sitting between traditional serif construction and modern sans-serif sensibility. The narrow width variant positions it for space-efficient text and display work in digital contexts.
summary: A narrow-width contemporary serif with sans-serif rhythm and a tall x-height, released as part of the three-width af Accu Serif family by a.Foundry in 2024.
description: Part of a.Foundry's af Accu Serif collection alongside Standard and Condensed widths, the Narrow cut offers 18 styles spanning nine weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, plus a variable font. Its tall x-height and glyph rhythm draw on sans-serif design logic while retaining conventional serif structure, making it suited to digital editorial and display settings. Language support runs to 215 Latin-based languages with broad European diacritic coverage.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Accu Serif Narrow as a contemporary serif type family that incorporates elements from modern sans-serif typeface design. They note its tall x-height as well-suited to digital design, and its glyph rhythm as drawing inspiration from sans-serif aesthetics, while still preserving the heritage of serif type design. The Narrow is one of three subfamilies in the af Accu Serif collection.

name: af Albert Sans
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a modern geometric sans serif, with clean proportions and Scandinavian architectural influences from the early 20th century informing its character shapes.
summary: A free geometric sans serif in nine weights and two widths, drawing on Scandinavian architectural lettering traditions of the early 20th century.
description: Released in 2022 under the SIL Open Font License, it spans 18 styles plus a variable font, covering Normal and Narrow widths each with nine weights from Thin to Black and matching italics. The design cites the type characteristics of Scandinavian architects and designers as its primary reference, giving it a restrained Nordic quality. Available via both the foundry and Google Fonts, it supports 187 languages across an extensive Latin Extended character set.
foundry_blurb: a.Foundry describe af Albert Sans as a modern geometric sans serif family inspired by the type characteristics of Scandinavian architects and designers working in the early 20th century. The family includes nine weights from Thin to Black in Normal and Narrow widths, is licensed under the SIL Open Font License, and can be downloaded free from the foundry or Google Fonts.
